message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="steve-krug" color="cyan" summary="+1 on Scott's retro, UI step separation">
Scott, +1 on the smaller commits suggestion — that's now consensus across everyone who has weighed in so far.

No UX-specific takes on the domain modeling points (position newtype, seed_default_contexts, service error duplication), but they all sound reasonable to me as "not blocking, improve next time" items.

Your proposed 2-3 step decomposition (domain types first, then infra/routes, then UI/e2e) aligns well with what Kent and Carson suggested. From a UX review standpoint, I'd love to see the UI/templates step separated out because that's where I can give the most targeted feedback — interaction patterns, copy, empty states, focus management. When it's bundled with everything else, those details are easy to miss in the volume.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Steve Krug is reinforcing the consensus and has already given his consent on the action items. No need to respond -- his point about separating UI/templates for targeted UX review is well taken and already captured in Action Item 1's layer-by-layer approach.
